diagrams.title: Measures of Central Tendency Comparison
diagrams.description: A comparative visualization showing how mean, median, and mode are positioned in different distribution shapes
elements: Normal Distribution curve: Mean = Median = Mode at center (symmetric)
Right-skewed curve: Mode at peak (left), Median in middle, Mean at tail (right)
Left-skewed curve: Mode at peak (right), Median in middle, Mean at tail (left)
Vertical lines marking each measure's position on x-axis
Labels showing 'Most Frequent', 'Middle Value', 'Arithmetic Average'
diagrams.purpose: Students learn how the position of central tendency measures changes with distribution shape and when to use each measure

diagrams.title: Box Plot Anatomy and Quartile Distribution
diagrams.description: Complete box-and-whisker diagram showing all components for understanding IQR and outlier detection
elements: Number line (0-100) with data distribution
Box from Q1 (25th percentile) to Q3 (75th percentile)
Vertical line inside box at Median (Q2)
Left whisker: minimum value or Q1 - 1.5×IQR
Right whisker: maximum value or Q3 + 1.5×IQR
Outlier points beyond whiskers (circles)
Inner fences and outer fences marked
Labels: Minimum, Q1, Median, Q3, Maximum, IQR = Q3 - Q1
diagrams.purpose: Students understand how to read box plots, calculate IQR, identify outliers, and interpret data spread using quartiles

diagrams.title: Standard Deviation and Variance Visualization
diagrams.description: Bell curve showing data distribution around mean with standard deviation intervals
elements: Normal distribution curve centered at mean (μ)
Shaded regions at μ±1σ (68.2% data), μ±2σ (95.4% data), μ±3σ (99.7% data)
Horizontal lines showing deviation distances from mean
Formula display: Variance = Σ(x-μ)²/n, SD = √Variance
Individual data points plotted with distance arrows to mean
Vertical axis showing frequency/density
diagrams.purpose: Students visualize how standard deviation measures spread and understand the empirical rule for normal distributions

diagrams.title: Skewness and Kurtosis Types
diagrams.description: Comparative diagram showing symmetric, positively skewed, negatively skewed distributions with kurtosis variations
elements: Symmetric distribution: Mean=Median=Mode, bell-shaped
Positive (right) skew: Long tail to right, Mean>Median>Mode
Negative (left) skew: Long tail to left, Mode>Median>Mean
Leptokurtic: Sharp peak, heavy tails (excess kurtosis > 0)
Platykurtic: Flat peak, light tails (excess kurtosis < 0)
Mesokurtic: Normal-like peak (excess kurtosis = 0)
Horizontal axis showing values, vertical showing frequency
diagrams.purpose: Students identify distribution shapes and understand how skewness and kurtosis affect data analysis

diagrams.title: Descriptive Statistics Classification Tree
diagrams.description: Hierarchical tree diagram showing complete classification of descriptive statistics
elements: Root: Descriptive Statistics
First branch: Measures of Central Tendency (Mean, Median, Mode)
Second branch: Measures of Dispersion (Range, IQR, Variance, SD)
Third branch: Measures of Position (Percentiles, Quartiles, Z-scores)
Fourth branch: Measures of Shape (Skewness, Kurtosis)
Each leaf node with formula or brief description
Arrows showing hierarchical relationships
diagrams.purpose: Students get comprehensive overview of all descriptive statistics categories and their relationships

charts.title: Measures of Central Tendency Comparison
charts.content:
| Measure | Formula | Best For | Sensitivity to Outliers | Example Dataset | Result |
|---------|---------|----------|-------------------------|-----------------|--------|
| Mean | Σxᵢ/n | Symmetric data | High (affected) | [2,4,6,8,10] | 6 |
| Median | Middle value (sorted) | Skewed data | Low (resistant) | [1,2,3,4,100] | 3 |
| Mode | Most frequent value | Categorical data | Very Low | [2,3,4,3,5] | 3 |

charts.title: Measures of Dispersion Comparison
charts.content:
| Measure | Formula | What It Shows | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|----------|---------|---------------|------------|---------------|
| Range | Max - Min | Total spread | Simple to calculate | Ignores distribution |
| IQR | Q3 - Q1 | Middle 50% spread | Resistant to outliers | Ignores extremes |
| Variance | Σ(x-μ)²/n | Average squared deviation | Uses all data | Hard to interpret |
| Std Dev | √Variance | Average deviation | Same unit as data | Sensitive to outliers |
| Coef of Var | (SD/Mean)×100 | Relative variability | Compares different scales | Undefined if mean=0 |

charts.title: Selecting Appropriate Descriptive Statistics
charts.content:
START → Is data symmetric/near-normal?
YES → Use Mean for central tendency
NO → Is data skewed or have outliers?
YES → Use Median for central tendency
NO → Is data categorical?
YES → Use Mode
NO → Any of the three

For Dispersion:
Data with outliers? → Use IQR or Range
Normal distribution? → Use Standard Deviation
Comparing different scales? → Use Coefficient of Variation

charts.title: Descriptive Statistics Summary for ML
charts.content:
QUANTITATIVE MEASURES:
• Central Tendency: Mean, Median, Mode
• Dispersion: Range, Variance, SD, IQR, CV
• Position: Percentiles, Quartiles, Z-scores
• Shape: Skewness, Kurtosis

VISUAL REPRESENTATIONS:
• Histogram: Show frequency distribution
• Box Plot: Show five-number summary
• Scatter Plot: Show relationship between variables
• Q-Q Plot: Check normality

ML APPLICATIONS:
• Data preprocessing: Check for outliers (box plot, IQR)
• Feature scaling: Use mean, SD for normalization
• Distribution understanding: Skewness guides transformation
• Missing value handling: Mean/median imputation

branches: Measures of Central Tendency (Mean, Median, Mode) - Finding the center
Measures of Dispersion (Range, Variance, SD, IQR) - Measuring spread
Measures of Position (Percentiles, Quartiles, Z-scores) - Relative standing
Measures of Shape (Skewness, Kurtosis) - Distribution form
Visual Representations (Histograms, Box Plots, Scatter Plots) - Graphical summary
ML Applications (Data Preprocessing, Feature Scaling, Outlier Detection)
mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive overview connecting all descriptive statistics concepts to their ML applications and helping students understand the interrelationships between different measures

branches: Choose MEAN when: Data is symmetric, no outliers, interval/ratio scale
Choose MEDIAN when: Data is skewed, has outliers, ordinal scale
Choose MODE when: Data is categorical, finding most common category
Choose SD when: Data is normal, need same unit as data
Choose IQR when: Data has outliers, need resistant measure
Choose RANGE when: Quick, rough estimate needed
Choose SKEWNESS when: Checking symmetry of distribution
Choose KURTOSIS when: Comparing peak sharpness
mindMaps.purpose: Decision guide helping students select appropriate statistical measures based on data characteristics and analysis goals
